EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE CITED IN PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES (TRANSACTION ID # 24091713) FILED BY DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF EXCEPT FOR NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING EXPOSURE AND CAUSATION. (DECLARATION OF PLUMMER, AY, AND DEFENDANT'S RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES.) THE COURT ALSO NOTES THAT DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NEED TO BE PLEAD VERY SPECIFICALLY. DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS OVERRULED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: BELDON YEE, CSR #2647

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Matthew S. Da Vega, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLC, (415) 898-1555; appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Andrea J. Casalett, of McKenna Long & Aldridge LLP (415) 267-4094; appearing for Defendant Plant Insulation and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Failure to shift burden of proof except for negligence cause of action. Triable issue of fact regarding exposure and causation. (Declaration of Plummer, Ay, and Defendant's responses to interrogatories.) The Court also notes that Defendant's special interrogatories need to be plead very specifically. Defendant's evidentiary objections overruled. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Beldon Yee, CSR 2647
